methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Hadrian is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Chris Power, currently valued at $1.6B post-money after raising $560M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 8 private robotics companies at same or adjacent stages, supplemented by 1 public comparable, including firms such as Nimble Robotics (Series C, $1.0B), Exotec (Series D, $2.0B), and Bedrock Robotics (Series B, $1.8B).

Two triangulation methods apply to this valuation. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier from Series C to the next funding stage, yielding an implied value of $2.7B based on the current $1.6B post-money. Peer-median analysis across the 8-company cohort in robotics and manufacturing produces a median valuation of $1.3B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide dispersion reflects material heterogeneity in stage and traction within the peer universe.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $1.1B (low) to $3.1B (high), with a median of $2.0B. This median sits materially above both the current post-money valuation and peer median, driven primarily by stage-progression methodology. Key uncertainties include the applicability of the 1.7x multiplier to Hadrian's specific risk profile and capital efficiency, and the representativeness of the peer set given the wide dispersion in valuations observed.
